message:
fix(transport): eliminate TOCTOU race in aggregator flush by using single write lock

The flush() method previously acquired a read lock to check whether
the flush interval had elapsed, then dropped it and acquired a separate
write lock to update the timestamp. Between these two acquisitions,
another thread could also pass the interval check and perform a
redundant flush, causing duplicate symbol emission and wasted work
under concurrent access.

Replace the read-then-write pattern with a single write lock acquisition
that both checks the interval and updates the timestamp atomically,
closing the race window entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
